name: First Community Bank Of Moultrie County
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank operating in Illinois with three local branches offering consumer loans, savings accounts, CDs, and IRAs with emphasis on personalized service.
description_long: First Community Bank of Moultrie County is a locally-owned and operated community bank serving the Moultrie County area in Illinois. The institution is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing customers with deposit protection up to regulatory limits. The bank emphasizes its community roots and locally-made decisions as core differentiators in a consolidated banking landscape. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), and consumer loans with competitive rates. They provide modern digital banking capabilities through online banking platforms and a mobile banking app, allowing customers to pay bills, check balances, and manage accounts remotely. The bank also offers online calculators for budgeting and educational resources including a monthly cyber education newsletter focused on customer security awareness. First Community Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on personal relationships and local decision-making rather than corporate bureaucracy. The bank operates three physical branch locations in Sullivan and Lovington, Illinois, with extended drive-up hours at the main branches to serve working customers. They maintain a strong security posture with spoof alerts educating customers about phishing attempts and emphasizing that the bank will not call asking for personal information. The primary limitation is geographic reach—the bank operates only in Illinois within a limited service area, making it inaccessible to customers outside Moultrie County or neighboring communities. While they offer competitive rates, the website does not provide specific APY figures or detailed loan terms, requiring customers to contact branches directly for pricing. As a small community bank, they may have fewer products and technological features compared to national banking chains, though this trade-off may suit customers prioritizing personalized service.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with higher rates of return
Online banking platform with bill payment functionality
Mobile banking app for remote account access
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full government backing provides regulatory deposit protection
Three physical branch locations with extended drive-up hours (some open until 5:30 PM weekdays and Saturday mornings)
Locally-owned with local decision-making authority rather than corporate approval processes
Mobile banking app available for remote account management and bill payment
Proactive security education including monthly cyber education newsletter for customers
Competitive deposit rates advertised on website with rate comparison tools
Wide range of consumer loan products with stated competitive rates
Online calculators and tools available for budget planning and financial management
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only three branches in Sullivan and Lovington, IL, inaccessible to customers outside Moultrie County region
Website does not display specific APY rates or loan terms; customers must contact branches directly for pricing information
Limited service hours at Lovington location (closed Thursdays before noon, closed some afternoons mid-week) may inconvenience some customers
Smaller institution may offer fewer specialized products, investment services, or advanced features compared to national banks
best_for: Moultrie County, Illinois residents seeking personalized service and local decision-making
Small business owners and farmers in central Illinois valuing community banking relationships
Customers prioritizing deposit security and FDIC insurance over expansive product offerings
diagnosis: First Community Bank of Moultrie County is best for Illinois residents in Moultrie County and surrounding areas who value personalized, locally-managed banking relationships and want FDIC-protected deposits. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—customers outside central Illinois cannot access this bank's services, and the institution lacks the product depth and technological sophistication of larger regional or national competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: FDIC-insured community bank in Illinois offering consumer loans, savings accounts, CDs, and IRAs.
fdic_official_name: First Community Bank of Moultrie County
